spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the headline CSS Name-only Container Queries surface (CSS, container-name, container-type); residual gaps in spec vocabulary: background-color.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button); controls include "Header zone"; "Sidebar zone"; "Content zone" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: One failure-mode signal is present but most edge cases (errors, unsupported environment, empty/zero inputs) are not demonstrated.

references.rationale: The concept page now includes the required canonical ChromeStatus self-heal link https://chromestatus.com/feature/5184267522015232.

design_system.rationale: Design-system regressions: raw hex colours present in <style>.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on sidebar-context
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Bring sidebar-context back onto the design system
openQuestions.detail: Design-system regressions: raw hex colours present in <style>. See CLAUDE.md rules #5, #6, #7 — CSS variables only, WCAG AA, button background+color always overridden together.

summary: sidebar-context covers the happy path of CSS Name-only Container Queries and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions. ChromeStatus reference link resolved.
